title: Sections of code should not be commented out
description: Sections of code should not be commented outAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEbased-on-misraunusedWhy is this an issue?More InfoCommented-out code distracts the focus from the actual executed code. It creates a noise that increases maintenance code. And because it is never executed, it quickly becomes out of date and invalid.Commented-out code should be deleted and can be retrieved from source control history if required.ExceptionsThis rule does not apply to code documentation using Doxygen, QDoc, markdown, or HTML tags.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAv
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Single-line commented-out code detected. Remove dead code - use version control for code history.
Commented-out function calls, assignments, or operations detected. Remove unused code.
Commented-out control flow statements detected. Remove dead code sections.
Commented-out preprocessor directives detected. Clean up unused includes and macros.
Commented-out code blocks or function calls detected. Remove unused implementations.
Multi-line commented-out code sections detected. Remove dead code blocks.
Multi-line commented code with assignments or directives detected. Clean up unused sections.
TODO/FIXME comments containing code patterns detected. Remove or implement the code.
